english: 4. **season**. Here Tibetans, of course, distinguish the four seasons of the temperate zone, _dpyid_ (dpyid) spring, _dbyar_ (dbyar) summer, _ston_ (ston) autumn, _dgun_ (dgun) winter; but in books, originally written in India, either three are counted, _tsá - dus_ (tsha dus) hot season, _gráń - dus_ (grang dus) cold season, _čár-dus_ (char dus) rainy season, or more accurately six: _dpyid_ (dpyid) (वसन्त) spring, i. e. March and April, _sos-kạ_ (sos ka) (ग्रीष्म) hot season, May, June, _dbyar_ (dbyar) (वर्ष) rainy season, July, August, _ston_ (ston) (शरत्) damp season, September, October, _dgun - stód_ (dgun stod) (हेमन्त) first part of winter, November, December, _dgun-smád_ (dgun smad) (शिशिर) last part of winter, January, February. — 5. **conjunctures, times, circumstances**, *_dus dé - mo_* (*dus de mo*) W., *_dhu - dí_* (*dhu di*) (_sa - ˳jám_) (sa 'jam) C., _dús-kyi k̊rúg-pa méd-pa_ (dus kyi 'khrug pa med pa) Ld.-Glr., _dus bzáń-po_ (dus bzang po) Dom., **peace**. — 6. **a particular period of time, as distinguished from others, an age**, युग (= 1000 कल्प), _yar-ldán_ (yar ldan), or _rdzogs-ldán_ (rdzogs ldan) (कृत or सत्य) _yar-rábs_ (yar rabs), or _gsum-ldán_ (gsum ldan) (त्रेता) _rtsod - ldán_ (rtsod ldan), or _gnyis - ldán_ (gnyis ldan) (द्वापर) _snyigs - ma_ (snyigs ma) (कलि), to be compared to the four ages of Greek mythology. — 7. **year** Lt. — 8. symb. num.: 6. — Note. _dus byéd-pa_ (dus byed pa) also signifies (cf. 1 above) to **fulfil the time**, _tsei dus byéd-pa_ (tshe'i dus byed pa) to **die, to perish**, also to commit **suicide** Dzl. frq.; _˳c̥i-baï dus byed-pa_ ('chi ba'i dus byed pa) id. Wdn. — _dus dzin-pa_ (dus 'dzin pa) to take the day-service upon one's self (?) Dzl. 282, 3. Comp. _dus-skabs_ (dus skabs) v. _skabs_. — _dus-čén,_ (dus chen) _-bzáń,_ (-bzang) _-stón,_ (-ston) **festival**, _byéd-pa_ (byed pa) to keep one. — _dus-mčód_ (dus mchod) v. _mčód-pa._ (mchod pa) — _dus-sbyor_ (dus sbyor) Cs.: **'judicial astrology'**, _dus-sbyor-pa_ (dus sbyor pa) an astrologer. — _dus-me_ (dus me) **comet** Cs. — _dus-rtsi-ba_ (dus rtsi ba) Cs. **'the counting of time'**. — _dus - tsig_ (dus tshig) Sch.: '_dus - tsig gsár - ba_ (dus tshig gsar ba) new, fresh provisions, **'produce of the year'** (?). — _dus-tsigs,_ (dus tshigs) _dus-mtsáms_ (dus mtshams) 1. **period, epoch**; 2. **season** Cs. — _dus - tsód_ (dus tshod) 1. **space or measure of time**. 2. often for _dus,_ (dus) _de._ (de) _dus-tsód-kyi mi-rnams_ (dus tshod kyi mi rnams) the men of that time or period, _deï dus-tsód - la_ (de'i dus tshod la) at that time; also for **hour**. — _dus-ziń_ (dus zhing) Sch.: **'time of depravity'**. — _dus-bzáń_ (dus bzang) v. above _dus-čén._ (dus chen) — _dus-rlabs_ (dus rlabs) **'wave of time'** i. e. **ebb and flood, the tides**, Stg. — _dus - lóg_ (dus log) a year yielding no crops, a **sterile, bad year** Pth.
1. num. figure: 101. — 2. affix of the gerund, for _te_ (te), after a final _d_ (d).

demonstrative pron. (in _B._ gen. placed after the word to which it belongs, in col. language before it, even without the termination of the genitive) **that, that one**, opp. to _˳di_ ('di) **this, this one**, yet with occasional exceptions. 1. when words or passages are literally quoted, the Tibetan begins with _˳di-skad_ ('di skad) or some similar expression, and places a _čes_ (ces) or _dé-skad_ (de skad) after it. _˳di,_ ('di) in such a case, corresponds about to **'the following'**, _de_ (de) to **'such'**, or **'thus'**, (cf. τοῦτο and τόδε). But elsewhere _˳di_ ('di) may also refer to what has been said before, e.g. in a reply: _tsig ˳di ni bdén - pa yin-nam_ (tshig 'di ni bden pa yin nam) is this word (that has just been said) true? _Dzl._ In the context of a narrative, however, _de_ (de) is usually employed. — 2. It frq. stands in the place of the definite article **the**: _p'a de lóg-ste sóń-ńo_ (pha de log ste song ngo) the father went back _Mil_; esp. after adjectives and participles, where it adds to perspicuity: _gźón - nu de na - ré_ (gzhon nu de na re) 'the younger one said _Mil._; _sńón-la sóń-ba de_ (sngon la song ba de) he that has gone on before _Mil._; _deï dón-du,_ (de'i don du) _deï p'yir(-du),_ (de'i phyir (du)) _čéd-du,_ (ched du) _slád-du,_ (slad du) **therefore, on this account, for this reason**; _deï ˳óg-tu_ (de'i 'og tu) **under that, after that, afterwards**; _deï dús-su,_ (de'i dus su) _tse(-na)_ (tshe (na)) **there, then, at that time.** — 3. **he, she, it**, for _k̊o,_ (kho) which in classical style is not in use. — 4. for _deï,_ (de'i) in _de-p'yir,_ (de phyir) _de-dus,_ (de dus) (abbreviations of _deï p'yir-du,_ (de'i phyir du) _deï dús-su,_ (de'i dus su) v. above). Plural: _dé-dag,_ (de dag) _dé-rnams,_ (de rnams) _dé-tso._ (de tsho) Comp. and deriv. _dé-ka,_ (de ka) _dé-k̊a,_ (de kha) **the very same**, _gsa dé - k̊a ńa yin_ (gsa' de kha nga yin) the very same snow-leopard (you saw) was I myself _Mil._; _dé - ka ltar_ (de ka ltar) **just so** _Thgy._; _dé - k̊a yod_ (de kha yod) (in answer to a question) **indeed! yes, yes! to be sure!** _Mil., C.,_ frq.; _dé - k̊a lags_ (de kha lags) _Mil.,_ id.; _de k̊yed lags_ (de khyed lags) _Pth.,_ oh, this . . . is you?! — _de-k̊ó-na,_ (de kho na) _de-nyíd,_ (de nyid) col. _de-ráń,_ (de rang) **the very same**, cf. _k̊ó-na;_ (kho na) _de - nyíd,_ (de nyid) and _de-k̊ó-na-nyíd_ (de kho na nyid) are also sbst.: **essence, nature** _Thgy._: _séms-kyi de-nyíd_ (sems kyi de nyid) the essence of the soul
